meta.styleTargetDiscrepancy: config.json contains malformed styleTarget text and points to a missing prose-sample.md. I used story-arc.md plus .afternoon/style-guide.json as the operative voice target and noted the mismatch.

examples: Around lines 141-143: 'lose the sequence back into the wind' -> 'lose the turn back into the wind' to keep Jaina's breakthrough bodily rather than note-like.
Around line 143: 'same watch lane' -> a concrete watch image ('in the corner of her eye and one turn from the bow').
Around line 301: 'the formation had settled that way anyway' -> 'the riders had settled that way anyway' to keep Sylvanas's diction field-native.
observations: The opener already matched the target's lean thriller cadence: short paragraphs, concrete camp labor, no throat-clearing recap.
Baelgun's voice stayed labor-first and dwarven-blunt; no smoothing was needed there.
Jaina's breakthrough section was the likeliest place for plan-language seep, so I checked the entire run from the bread-smell through 'Three schools.'
Sylvanas's road section largely held the right dry pressure, but one use of 'formation' sounded more outline-ish than lived.
Dialogue tag use was already disciplined; the chapter leans mostly on said, bare lines, and action beats rather than decorative tags.

notes: No hard limited-third breach found; the chapter stays inside Jaina for scenes 1-3 and Sylvanas for scenes 4-5.
observations: I checked the Jaina/Kel'Thuzad reflection stretch around lines 89-103; it stays inside what Jaina would think, fear, and keep doing.
I checked the breakthrough cluster around lines 121-149; the prose stays with Jaina's bodily reasoning rather than an external narrator explaining the theorem.
I checked the ship-stone exchange around lines 179-213; Antonidas is heard only through Jaina's reception of the call.
I checked the village and relay run around lines 303-348; Sylvanas's anger stays tactical and sensory, not omniscient.
I checked the palace-side planning around lines 418-455; the prose infers danger from what Sylvanas sees and knows rather than announcing unseen facts.

examples: Around lines 187-190 and 327-340: Antonidas's timeline now says Arthas entered the city three days ago so the relay's four-day palace update remains a real worsening instead of a contradiction.
observations: The opener carries chapter21 cleanly: Muradin arrives alive, wounded, and immediately in triage rather than being reintroduced from zero.
Jaina's bread-smell and tremor are treated as worsened baseline, matching chapter20-21 memory rather than re-revealing the symptom.
Daggercap Bay is handled as callback material: burned docks, wreckage, tar, and old char instead of a fresh scenic introduction.
Cyndia's widened protection around Jaina stays behavioral and unnamed, which matches the plan's handoff rule for chapter23.
Antonidas, Uther, and the warning lane all read as continuing threads, not surprise rescue infrastructure.

examples: Around line 253: 'speak the sequence aloud' -> 'speak the steps aloud' for a cleaner beat and less abstract repetition.
Around line 455: collapsed a tactical note-run into one sentence so the service-arch paragraph lands as pressure, not a bullet-point map.
observations: The chapter's clipped motion generally suits the endgame voice; I did not lengthen the march or deck-labor chains just to vary them artificially.
The opener's short-sentence pressure works because the forge, hide flap, and triage sounds keep the rhythm anchored in matter.
The take/throw sequence in Jaina's mind works as intended because the fragments are surrounded by longer explanatory sentences.
The road-south section needed the most attention for cadence flattening; I checked the village, relay, and Antonidas-call stretch for too many short beats in a row.
The chapter remains within style-guide thresholds for simile pressure: one 'like' and two 'as if' constructions in the whole draft.

examples: Cross-chapter opening: chapter21's Muradin carry-out now lands cleanly in chapter22's camp handoff with no recap seam.
Back-half threshold: the palace preview now stops at open exposure and sword range instead of storyboard-level room geometry, preserving chapter23's discovery pressure.
continuity.crossChapter: Verified against chapter21/final.md; coherent direct carry.
observations: Scene 1 begins in immediate consequence, not summary, which honors the plan's chapterBridge.
The Daggercap-to-Wolfheart movement stays causal: camp dismissal leads straight to the bay and ship turnabout.
The Southshore hard cut is earned because landfall gives no relief and the first road image immediately reorients the reader inside Lordaeron.
The relay runner materially worsens the timetable rather than repeating Antonidas's earlier warning after the timeline fix.
The service-door close cleanly ends public help and hands chapter23 the correct pressure state.

examples: Around line 344: 'Something inside the palace has turned' -> 'The palace is wrong inside' to cut vague abstract phrasing in a high-pressure message.
Around line 455: removed tactical-overview drift that was reading more like planning shorthand than finished scene prose.
observations: I re-checked the opener for threshold metaphors after the grounder notes; the hide-flap, bucket, forge, and shelter sounds still carry the scene concretely.
I re-checked the ship section for leftover abstraction after the slophunter pass; the deck labor, blood, rope, and fish keep it material.
The celebration villages already do the moral work through ribbons, painted boards, and civilian behavior; no extra sermonizing was added.
The final arch and service door now end on motion and cutoff, not on explanatory summary.
No scene-opening cliché survived strongly enough to warrant a fresh cut; each scene opens on labor, weather, or problem-in-motion rather than generic waking/weather throat-clearing.

examples: Around line 426: 'Fire changes the binding while it's bare' -> 'Fire turns the binding against itself while it's bare' to keep Jaina's field explanation sharp and lived rather than abstractly technical.
observations: Baelgun's speech stays plain, local, and labor-first; it never slips into institutional phrasing.
Dagny's captain voice stays concrete and coercive in the right way: blankets, lines, corpses, deck, food.
Antonidas sounds tired and exact, not bureaucratic; his compression reads mentor-pressure, not memo language.
Sylvanas's spoken lines remain clipped and plain even when she is issuing covert instructions on the road.
I scanned quoted speech for the zero-tolerance offenders in the hitlist; none of the banned report-words remained in dialogue.
